llm_explanation: Program rules require that every person applying for benefits be either a U.S. citizen or a qualified noncitizen, such as a lawful permanent resident or refugee. Because one household member does not meet these citizenship or immigration criteria, the household cannot be approved for participation in the program.

satisfied.text: A household must reside within the administering state to qualify for participation.
For categorically eligible PA households, the county welfare department shall not require verification of gross income.
For categorically eligible PA households, the county welfare department shall not require verification of net income.
Resource transfers shall not be accepted without verification for categorically eligible households.
Sponsored noncitizens shall cooperate in providing sponsor information as specified in Section 63-405.7.
Thirty percent of the household's net food stamp income shall be deducted from the reduced Thrifty Food Plan amount.
Households whose gross income exceeds the established limit are ineligible for benefits.
Households whose net income exceeds the limit are ineligible for participation.
If the gross income factor is accepted without verification for categorically eligible PA households, it shall not affect eligibility.
The CWD shall determine whether good cause exists upon finding that an applicant voluntarily quit or reduced work hours.
Applicants who voluntarily quit or reduce work effort without good cause are ineligible for benefits.
Resources are accepted without verification for categorically eligible households.
Resource transfers must be verified to determine eligibility.
If the household transfers resources but the agency fails to verify them, eligibility determination violates policy.
Medical expenses claimed as deductions must be verified by the county welfare department before application.
Medical expenses exceeding allowable limits or not verified shall not be deducted from income.
Countable income after medical deductions is the household's total income minus allowable medical deductions.
Shelter deductions for households without elderly or disabled members shall not exceed the maximum shelter cap amount.
Shelter expenses that exceed the allowable cap amount are not fully deductible.

violated.text: A household must reside in the county in which it files an application for participation.
No individual may participate as a member of more than one household or county in any month except for eligible residents of shelters for battered women and children.
Households whose gross income does not exceed the established limit meet the gross income test for eligibility.
Households with both gross and net income at or below the respective limits are eligible for participation.
Households containing an elderly or disabled member are entitled to a deduction for verified medical expenses exceeding $35 per month.
If an elderly or disabled household has verified medical expenses above the threshold, the deduction shall be applied in computing net income.
Allowable medical expenses include prescription drugs, medical supplies, dental care, health insurance premiums, and costs for nursing services.
